groupSummary: This group focuses on claims regarding the misinformation linking vaccines to autism, particularly in relation to changes in CDC messaging.

claims.claimText: The CDC has quietly changed its vaccines & autism page, softening decades of scientific clarity.
claims.postExcerpt: This is a warning sign for every healthcare system, including the NHS.

claims.claimText: A years-long campaign to falsely link vaccines and autism has evolved from online conspiracy to federal guidance.
claims.postExcerpt: reshaping the CDC, weakening disease surveillance, and endangering public health.

claims.claimText: Public health experts are expressing significant concerns about widespread confusion stemming from a new CDC claim suggesting that vaccines cannot be definitively ruled out as an autism cause.
claims.postExcerpt: significant concerns about widespread confusion.

claims.claimText: The CDC’s new claim that vaccines cannot be ruled out as a cause of autism will lead to years of confusion and vaccine refusal.
claims.postExcerpt: years of confusion and vaccine refusal.

claims.claimText: Harmful myths suggesting that vaccines cause autism sow fear and distrust in vaccines.
claims.postExcerpt: jeopardizing the great progress we’ve made in preventing serious childhood diseases.

claims.claimText: Misinformation about Hepatitis B and the shot has grown rampant in recent years, largely thanks to anti-vaccine activists.
claims.postExcerpt: thanks to anti-vaccine activists such as Kennedy.

claims.claimText: Governor Maura Healey warned of misinformation about the childhood vaccine schedule, especially the infant hepatitis B vaccine.
claims.postExcerpt: ahead of a pivotal meeting of federal vaccine advisors.

claims.claimText: Public health experts try to ‘prebunk’ misinformation about vaccines ahead of CDC vaccine meeting.
claims.postExcerpt: filled with misinformation and vaccine myths.

claims.claimText: A total farce occurred at the CDC advisory meeting regarding vaccine misinformation.
claims.postExcerpt: Lawyer with ties to Kennedy sparks outcry.

claims.claimText: RFK Jr. claims there’s no proof that vaccines don’t cause autism, which is a lie.
claims.postExcerpt: he’s personally responsible for misinformation.
